states_list.name: INIT
states_list.description: Initial state after connection, awaiting user identity.

states_list.name: AUTH_PENDING
states_list.description: User identity provided, awaiting password verification.

states_list.name: AUTHENTICATED
states_list.description: Successful login, session ready for commands.

states_list.name: DATA_CHANNEL_READY
states_list.description: Data connection parameters negotiated (Passive/Active), ready for transfer command.

states_list.name: DATA_TRANSFER
states_list.description: File or directory listing transfer in progress.

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: Rename source accepted, waiting for destination path.

invariants_list.rule: The PASS command must be sent immediately following a USER command.
Authentication (USER and PASS) must be completed successfully before data transfer commands like LIST, RETR, or STOR can be executed.
A data connection establishment command (PASV or PORT) must be issued prior to any command that requires a data connection (LIST, RETR, STOR, etc.).
The QUIT command can be sent at any time to terminate the session, regardless of the current state.
If authentication fails (e.g., response 530 to PASS), the client may restart the sequence by sending a new USER command.
The RNTO command must follow an RNFR command to complete a file renaming operation.
Data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, etc.) involve a 1xx response followed by a 2xx response upon completion.
CWD.path: Must be a valid path on the server filesystem
RETR.filename: Must specify a file to retrieve
STOR.filename: Must specify a file name to store
RNFR.filename: Must specify the file to be renamed
RNTO.filename: Must specify the new name for the file

recommended_actions: Next iteration should emphasize broader data-channel and post-auth file-operation seeds to improve message-to-transition coverage.

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

status_changes.rationale: First step of FTP authentication. Response code 331 is correctly noted.
Completion of FTP authentication with response 230.
Auth failure (530) often keeps the state in an authentication-required phase, but some servers require USER again. Marks as hypothesis.
Can happen if default connection used or previous PASV/PORT state persists, though usually follows a channel setup. 150 response suggests transfer started.
Standard sequence for retrieving files. 150 response.
Standard sequence for storing files. 150 response.
REIN command resets the session state.
IPv6 extension for passive mode setup.
IPv6 extension for active mode setup.
Normally requires data channel setup first. 150 response in trace suggests transfer, but state logic may be incomplete.
Normally requires data channel setup first.
Normally requires data channel setup first.
Normally requires data channel setup first.
Standard transfer using established channel. 150 response.
Standard transfer using established channel. 150 response.
Standard transfer using established channel. 150 response.
Returns to control state after transfer completion.
Returns to control state after transfer completion.
Returns to control state after transfer completion.
Returns to control state after transfer completion.

status_changes.description: The PASS command must be sent immediately following a USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP protocol requirement for successful login sequence.

status_changes.description: If authentication fails (e.g., response 530 to PASS), the client may restart the sequence by sending a new USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Standard error recovery path in FTP authentication.

status_changes.description: CWD.path: Must be a valid path on the server filesystem
status_changes.rationale: Functional requirement, though the protocol technically allows sending invalid paths (resulting in error codes).
